phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 48.6 dBA, gradually settling to 28.6-37.6 dBA midway through session. One or more speech segments identified (avg confidence 0.6), suggesting quiet setting with occasional voices. Overall environment is exceptionally quiet, highly conducive to concentration.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 76 bpm as session average. This aligns with ideal focus-state parameters.
phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (5.3m) that improves to 1.4m as session progresses. Kinetic data shows user establishing stable position within first seconds; minimal body movement thereafter.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) corresponds to kinetic patterns. Acoustic consistency observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session presents suboptimal focus conditions: elevated ambient noise (37.6 dB), transitional positioning. Concentration likely impacted.

phase_3.recommendations: Current 54-second duration too brief for meaningful concentration assessment; aim for 10+ min sessions.

reason: Concentration score reflects poor audio conditions (37.6dB), minimal motion, and brief session length.
